sanskrit_devanagari:
अर्जुन उवाच
अथ केन प्रयुक्तोऽयं पापं चरति पूरुषः ।
अनिच्छन्नपि वार्ष्णेय बलादिव नियोजितः ॥ ३६ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
arjuna uvāca
atha kena prayukto 'yaṁ
pāpaṁ carati pūruṣaḥ |
anicchann api vārṣṇeya
balād iva niyojitaḥ || 36 ||

translations.verbatim_quote: Arjuna said: O descendant of Vṛṣṇi, by what is one impelled to sinful acts, even unwillingly, as if engaged by force?
(See holy-bhagavad-gita.org for full Mukundananda rendering; agreement with Prabhupada noted in disagreements section where it diverges materially.)

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: A living entity, as part and parcel of the Supreme, is originally spiritual, pure, and free from all material contaminations. Therefore, by nature he is not subject to the sins of the material world.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.36 Atha, now then; varsneya, O scion of the Vrsni dynasty; being prayuktah, impelled; kena, by what acting as the cause; as a servant is by a king, does ayam, this; purusah, man; carati, commit; papam, sin, a sinful act; api, even; anicchan, against his wish, though not himself
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Shankara: Arjuna asks the practical question following from 3.34. atha — now — kena prayuktaḥ — by what is one impelled — pāpaṁ carati pūruṣaḥ — does the man perform sinful action — anicchann api — even unwillingly — vārṣṇeya — O descendant of Vṛṣṇi — balād iva niyojitaḥ — as if engaged by force. The question's structural force: the actor experiences his own sinful action as compelled, not as chosen; what is this compelling agent?

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.36 Arjuna said — Impelled by what does a man practising Jnana Yoga commit sin in the form of experiencing the objects of the senses, as if constrained by force, even against his own will not to experience the objects of the senses.
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Ramanuja: Arjuna, having heard the structural diagnosis of 3.34 (rāga and dveṣa seated in sense-object encounter), asks the practical question: what is the deeper agent that drives the seeker toward sinful action even when his explicit will is opposed? The 'as if forced' (balād iva) phrasing identifies the experienced compulsion. The next verse (3.37) will name kāma.

positions.rendering: by what is one impelled to sinful acts, even unwillingly, as if engaged by force?
what is it that propels a person to sinful actions, even unwillingly, as if compelled by force?
the question identifies the puzzle: the actor experiences the sinful action as if forced (balād iva), against his own will; what is the agent of this experienced compulsion?
Arjuna's question follows from 3.34: if rāga and dveṣa are seated in the sense-object encounter, what is the deeper agent that drives the actor toward sin even against his explicit volition?

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: Translators capture the surface but sometimes lose the doctrinal pivot — Arjuna is asking specifically about the experienced compulsion (the 'as if forced' is doing structural work, not just emphatic). Krishna's answer at 3.37 names the agent (kāma-krodha) that produces this experience.

literal_meaning: Arjuna said: But by what is one impelled to commit sin, even unwilling, O Vārṣṇeya, as if forced?
traditional_meaning_consensus: Arjuna's direct question that sets up the kāma-analysis of 3.37-3.43. He acknowledges the structural insight Krishna has just given (3.34: rāga and dveṣa as obstacles) and asks the practical question: what is the agent that drives the actor to sinful action even when the actor does not will it? balād iva niyojitaḥ — as if engaged by force. The verse establishes the chapter's closing puzzle that Krishna will answer in 3.37.
